title: Microsoft Outlook Add-In
excerpt: Jira, Wrike, and ClickUp all have add-ins/plug-ins for Microsoft Outlook that allow a user to send an email or other item (e.g., calendar invite) to a particular task/issue using a graphical user interface / point-and-cl…

posts.author: packwood (Joshua Packwood)
posts.cookedHtml:
Jira, Wrike, and ClickUp all have add-ins/plug-ins for Microsoft Outlook that allow a user to send an email or other item (e.g., calendar invite) to a particular task/issue using a graphical user interface / point-and-click. It would be very helpful Monday.com to create similar Outlook web, iOS mobile / Android, and desktop add-ins/plug-ins.

I am aware that a user could forward an email to a special email address that would associate it with a project. However, that would require the user to know the email address for each project as opposed to a point-and-click user interface directly in Outlook that would allow them to search for projects and then tasks which would be much easier for most users.

posts.author: RussK (Russ K)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed. Other key players, like Hubspot, etc. have this functionality. If Monday.com wants to compete in the CRM space, this functionality is critical. It allows a user to easily control what gets logged to Monday and which contact/lead/deal it should be associated with at a click.

posts.author: AndrewPike.Fogo (Andrew Pike)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a real pain point for my team as I try to onboard with Monday.com to manage my department’s task list. We run a helpdesk and have tons of email communication. Having to look up the address for each task in Monday, then forward the communication is a big inconvenience.
